(f) The LALR(1) parsing table is the same as the SLR(1) parsing table shown in (c).
We use similar language to that on page 210, with appropriate modifi cations:
The SLR(l) parsing algorithm. Let s be the current state whose number is at the top of the parsing stack. Then actions are defined as follows:

(1) If state s contains any item of the form A → α.X β, where X is a terminal, and X is the next token in the input string, then the action is to remove the current input token and push onto the stack the number of the state containing the item A → αX. β.
